_note: Curated canon of papers that changed the course of computing. Enriched at build time with live OpenAlex citation counts via knowledge_os/landmarks.py.

landmarks.title: On Computable Numbers, with an Application to the Entscheidungsproblem
landmarks.why: Defined computation itself — the Turing machine, and the limits of what any machine can compute.

landmarks.title: A Mathematical Theory of Communication
landmarks.why: Founded information theory and gave us the bit — the basis of all digital communication and storage.

landmarks.title: Computing Machinery and Intelligence
landmarks.why: Posed the question 'can machines think?' and framed it as the imitation game (the Turing test).

landmarks.title: A Note on Two Problems in Connexion with Graphs
landmarks.why: Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm — still routing the internet and your GPS today.

landmarks.title: An Algorithm for the Machine Calculation of Complex Fourier Series
landmarks.why: The Fast Fourier Transform — made digital signal processing practical across all of engineering.

landmarks.title: Go To Statement Considered Harmful
landmarks.why: Launched structured programming and the discipline of writing comprehensible software.

landmarks.title: A Relational Model of Data for Large Shared Data Banks
landmarks.why: Invented the relational database — the foundation of essentially every business system since.

landmarks.title: The Complexity of Theorem-Proving Procedures
landmarks.why: Defined NP-completeness — the line between tractable and intractable problems (P vs NP).

landmarks.title: Reducibility Among Combinatorial Problems
landmarks.why: Showed 21 classic problems are NP-complete, mapping the landscape of computational hardness.

landmarks.title: A Protocol for Packet Network Intercommunication
landmarks.why: TCP/IP — the protocol that became the Internet.

landmarks.title: New Directions in Cryptography
landmarks.why: Invented public-key cryptography — how strangers exchange secrets without meeting.

landmarks.title: A Method for Obtaining Digital Signatures and Public-Key Cryptosystems
landmarks.why: RSA — the public-key cryptosystem that secures the web.

landmarks.title: Time, Clocks, and the Ordering of Events in a Distributed System
landmarks.why: Logical clocks — the conceptual foundation of distributed systems.

landmarks.title: How to Share a Secret
landmarks.why: Secret sharing — split a secret so any k of n parts reconstruct it, fewer reveal nothing.

landmarks.title: The Byzantine Generals Problem
landmarks.why: Defined fault tolerance against arbitrary/malicious failures — later the heart of blockchains.

landmarks.title: A Theory of the Learnable
landmarks.why: PAC learning — gave machine learning a rigorous theoretical foundation.

landmarks.title: Impossibility of Distributed Consensus with One Faulty Process
landmarks.why: The FLP impossibility — a hard limit every consensus protocol since has had to route around.

landmarks.title: Learning representations by back-propagating errors
landmarks.why: Backpropagation — the algorithm that trains essentially every neural network.

landmarks.title: Information Management: A Proposal
landmarks.why: The blueprint for the World Wide Web.

landmarks.title: The Anatomy of a Large-Scale Hypertextual Web Search Engine
landmarks.why: PageRank and the architecture of Google — organized the web.

landmarks.title: The Part-Time Parliament
landmarks.why: Paxos — the consensus algorithm running the world's databases and cloud control planes.

landmarks.title: Gradient-Based Learning Applied to Document Recognition
landmarks.why: Convolutional neural networks (LeNet) — the template for modern computer vision.

landmarks.title: Random Forests
landmarks.why: A robust, general-purpose ML method that dominated tabular prediction for a decade.

landmarks.title: MapReduce: Simplified Data Processing on Large Clusters
landmarks.why: Made big-data processing across thousands of machines simple — launched the data era.

landmarks.title: A Fast Learning Algorithm for Deep Belief Nets
landmarks.why: Reignited deep learning and showed deep networks could be trained.

landmarks.title: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
landmarks.why: Solved decentralized consensus among untrusting strangers — blockchain and a new asset class.

landmarks.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
landmarks.why: AlexNet — the result that started the deep-learning revolution.

landmarks.title: Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space
landmarks.why: word2vec — learned word embeddings, the idea that meaning can be geometry.

landmarks.title: Generative Adversarial Networks
landmarks.why: GANs — two networks competing to generate realistic data; launched modern generative AI.

landmarks.title: Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks
landmarks.why: Seq2seq — neural networks that map sequences to sequences; the basis of neural translation.

landmarks.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
landmarks.why: Adam — the default optimizer that trains most deep-learning models.

landmarks.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
landmarks.why: ResNet — residual connections let networks go hundreds of layers deep.

landmarks.title: Attention Is All You Need
landmarks.why: The Transformer — the architecture behind every modern large language model, including this one.

landmarks.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
landmarks.why: Pretrained language models — fine-tune one big model for everything; reshaped NLP.

landmarks.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
landmarks.why: GPT-3 — showed that scale alone yields in-context learning; the template for modern AI.

landmarks.title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
landmarks.why: AI solved a 50-year grand-challenge in biology — protein structure prediction.
